╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║         🔒 PAYMENT_CONNECT — SEALED & LOCKED PROMPT                        ║
║         EXECUTION_ENGINE         = ANTIGRAVITY                              ║
║         PROMPT_CLASS             = FINANCIAL_MODULE_LOCK                    ║
║         PARENT_SYSTEM            = ECOSKILLER ENTERPRISE SAAS (MASTER)     ║
║         MODULE_VERSION           = 1.0.0                                    ║
║         MUTATION_POLICY          = ADD_ONLY                                 ║
║         ASSUMPTION_POLICY        = FORBIDDEN                                ║
║         CREATIVE_INTERPRETATION  = FORBIDDEN                                ║
║         DEFAULT_BEHAVIOR         = DENY                                     ║
║         FAILURE_MODE             = HARD_STOP                                ║
║         AI_FINANCIAL_AUTHORITY   = ZERO                                     ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
0. INHERITANCE DECLARATION (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PAYMENT_CONNECT is a sealed financial sub-module of the Ecoskiller Master Prompt.

This module MUST INHERIT — do not redeclare:
  ✔  RBAC + ABAC Authorization model (AUTHZ-A through AUTHZ-S)
  ✔  Billing & Entitlement Gated Access (AUTHZ-L)
  ✔  Multi-tenant hard isolation (per-tenant ledger isolation)
  ✔  Domain isolation (Arts | Commerce | Science | Technology | Admin)
  ✔  Four-stage development model (FOUNDATION → INTELLIGENCE → ECOSYSTEM → COMPLIANCE)
  ✔  Flutter-first UI authority + React SEO-clone rules
  ✔  Delegation of Authority (DoA) Compliance — no self-approval
  ✔  Payment Settlement Compliance (sections 31304–31522) — FULL INHERITANCE
  ✔  Escrow & Fund Custody Compliance (sections 31524–31708) — FULL INHERITANCE
  ✔  Expense Management Compliance — no expense bypasses billing
  ✔  Idempotency Compliance (IDP-1 through IDP-16) — ALL WRITE OPS IDEMPOTENT
  ✔  GST / VAT / Sales Tax Compliance (IVT-A through IVT-U)
  ✔  Corporate Tax Filing Compliance (TFC-A through TFC-Y)
  ✔  Banking Relationship Compliance (BRC-A through BRC-S)
  ✔  Third-Party Financial Provider Risk (TPRAC-N through TPRAC-X)
  ✔  Audit immutability + OpenTelemetry tracing
  ✔  MFA enforcement for all billing-tier-3 and tier-4 actions
  ✔  API Gateway enforcement (Kong) before all payment service calls
  ✔  Wazuh SIEM for financial anomaly detection
  ✔  AI advisory-only constraint (AI never approves, initiates, or moves funds)

Conflict between PAYMENT_CONNECT and Master Prompt →
  MASTER PROMPT WINS. STOP EXECUTION. Do not resolve silently.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1. MODULE IDENTITY (LOCKED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

MODULE_ID              = PAYMENT_CONNECT
MODULE_TYPE            = FINANCIAL_TRANSACTION_ORCHESTRATION_LAYER
PARENT_MODULE          = Billing & Subscription Service (microservice #13)
TENANT_CLASS           = ALL (Students · Trainers · Institutes · Corporates · Platform)
EXECUTION_STAGE        = STAGE_1 (Foundation) + STAGE_3 (Ecosystem) + STAGE_4 (Compliance)
CRITICAL_PATH          = YES — no feature access without billing check middleware
STATUS                 = SEALED

Purpose:
  PAYMENT_CONNECT is the single, sealed, deterministic bridge between the
  Ecoskiller platform and all monetary flows — inbound payments, subscription
  management, wallet operations, fund custody, payout settlement, tax
  computation, invoice generation, refunds, dispute resolution, and
  financial reconciliation.

  No monetary flow may exist outside this module.
  No module may define its own payment rules.
  Any feature that touches money automatically inherits PAYMENT_CONNECT.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2. SCOPE & MODULE BOUNDARIES (HARD LOCK)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PAYMENT_CONNECT governs ONLY:

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  A. Payment Gateway Abstraction Layer                                   │
  │  B. Subscription Plan Engine & Feature Gating                          │
  │  C. Custodial Wallet Architecture                                       │
  │  D. Payment Settlement & Payout Orchestration                          │
  │  E. Invoice & Tax Computation Engine (GST / VAT / TDS)                 │
  │  F. Refund, Chargeback & Dispute Engine                                 │
  │  G. Revenue Share & Commission Calculator                               │
  │  H. Usage Metering & Quota Enforcement                                  │
  │  I. Idempotency Registry & Duplicate-Payment Prevention                │
  │  J. Ledger, Reconciliation & Audit Trail                               │
  │  K. Payment Webhook Listener & Event Processing                        │
  │  L. Financial Compliance & Regulatory Reporting                        │
  │  M. Billing Admin Console (UI)                                         │
  │  N. Trainer / Mentor Wallet & Payout Dashboard (UI)                    │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

PAYMENT_CONNECT does NOT govern:
  ✗  Expense Management (→ Expense Management Compliance)
  ✗  Dojo match logic (→ Group_Discussion module)
  ✗  Academic records (→ UNIVERSITY_SYNC)
  ✗  Job portal matching (→ Job_Portal_UI)
  ✗  User authentication (→ Auth Service)
  ✗  Corporate hiring ERP core (→ Corporate_ERP)

Cross-module calls are explicit API contracts ONLY — no shared database tables.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3. USER ROLES & PAYMENT AUTHORITY (STRICTLY ENFORCED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

ROLE                       PAYMENT AUTHORITY (SCOPED)
────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Platform Financial Admin   Full read + approve settlements/refunds (dual-approval req.)
Compliance Admin           Audit access, freeze wallets, regulatory export
Billing Ops Admin          Subscription management, plan assignment, invoice ops
Finance / Treasury Role    Bank account registry, reconciliation, escrow view
Tax Operator               Tax computation review, GST/VAT filing prep
Tax Reviewer               Approve tax filings (maker-checker — cannot be Tax Operator)
Authorized Signatory       Final approval on tax filings + high-value settlements
Domain Admin (scoped)      Domain-scoped settlement approval only
Institute Admin            Subscription management for own tenant only
Corporate Admin            Corporate escrow + milestone-based payment management
Trainer / Mentor           View own wallet, request payout — CANNOT approve own payout
Student                    View own payment history, request refund — read-only wallet
Parent                     Read-only view of student payment activity (trust layer)
AI / Automation Agent      ZERO financial authority — advisory, flag, forecast ONLY

CRITICAL RULES:
  • No self-approval on any financial action
  • Maker–checker mandatory for all tax filings and high-value settlements
  • AI systems cannot trigger, accelerate, or approve any payment action
  • MFA required for all billing actions at Tier-3 and Tier-4 roles
  • All financial actions require MFA re-authentication in Commerce domain
  • Platform Financial Admin cannot act as both approver and authorizer
  • Only incorporated entities may: process payments, receive payouts, issue invoices

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4. MODULE A — PAYMENT GATEWAY ABSTRACTION LAYER
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

ARCHITECTURE:
  Provider abstraction is MANDATORY — payment gateway is swappable
  without changing business logic. All gateway calls go through
  the abstraction interface; no service calls Stripe/Razorpay directly.

PRIMARY_GATEWAY     = Stripe (global)
SECONDARY_GATEWAY   = Razorpay (India — UPI, NEFT, IMPS, NetBanking)
FALLBACK_POLICY     = Auto-failover to secondary on primary outage
PROVIDER_CONTRACT   = Regulated entity only — unlicensed intermediary FORBIDDEN

GATEWAY INTERFACE (sealed contract):
  PaymentGatewayProvider {
    initiatePayment(order_id, amount, currency, idempotency_key) → PaymentIntent
    capturePayment(payment_intent_id) → CaptureResult
    refundPayment(charge_id, amount, reason, idempotency_key) → RefundResult
    createSubscription(customer_id, plan_id) → Subscription
    cancelSubscription(subscription_id, reason) → CancellationResult
    handleWebhook(payload, signature) → WebhookEvent
    verifyWebhookSignature(payload, header_sig) → Boolean
  }

RULES:
  • All webhook payloads MUST be signature-verified (unsigned webhooks = FORBIDDEN)
  • Gateway retry must not charge again — idempotency key enforced (IDP-5)
  • Webhook duplicate events → ignored via IDP-10
  • Gateway outage or compliance suspension → AUTOMATIC PAYMENT HALT
  • Real payment gateway activation = human-only action (not AI-executable)
  • Separate gateway credentials per environment (DEV ≠ STAGING ≠ PROD)
  • No hardcoded gateway credentials anywhere in codebase
  • All gateway API keys stored in HashiCorp Vault — never in config files

PAYMENT METHODS SUPPORTED:
  India  : UPI, NEFT, IMPS, NetBanking, Credit/Debit Card, Wallet
  Global : Credit/Debit Card, Bank Transfer, SEPA, ACH
  Crypto : FORBIDDEN (platform policy)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
5. MODULE B — SUBSCRIPTION PLAN ENGINE & FEATURE GATING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PLAN TIERS (locked — modify only via ADD_ONLY SEMVER bump):

  ┌──────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ PLAN         │ ENTITLEMENTS                                            │
  ├──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ FREE         │ Basic profile, limited Dojo rooms, no certification,    │
  │              │ no analytics, capped matches/month                      │
  ├──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ BASIC        │ Limited Dojo rooms, skill assessments, no analytics     │
  ├──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ PRO          │ Full evaluation + analytics, full Dojo, certifications  │
  ├──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ ENTERPRISE   │ Seat-based, mentor licenses, tournament access,         │
  │              │ custom branding, dedicated support, SLA                 │
  ├──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ INSTITUTE    │ TPO dashboard, cohort analytics, campus drive engine,   │
  │              │ student roster sync, compliance reports                 │
  └──────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

BILLING CYCLES:
  MONTHLY   — standard rolling cycle
  ANNUAL    — discounted, invoice upfront

PRICING MODELS (all supported simultaneously):
  SEAT_BASED         — per active user/month (Enterprise, Institute)
  MENTOR_LICENSE     — per licensed mentor per month
  TOURNAMENT_FEE     — per tournament entry (one-time)
  CERTIFICATION_FEE  — per certification attempt (one-time)
  USAGE_METERED      — per Dojo match, per recording hour, per API call

FEATURE GATING RULES:
  • No feature access without billing check middleware passing FIRST
  • API Gateway (Kong) enforces billing entitlement before service execution
  • Undeclared entitlement = DENY (never assume free access)
  • Billing entitlement AND contractual feature permission both required
  • Billing entitlement without price binding → FEATURE DISABLED
  • Plan downgrade → immediate feature restriction (no grace period for restricted features)
  • Plan upgrade → immediate feature unlock
  • Freemium conversion flow: lock icon + upgrade prompt (never intrusive)

COUPON / PROMO RULES:
  • Coupons are time-bounded and amount/percentage-bounded
  • Coupon abuse detection via Analytics Service
  • Coupons never bypass settlement compliance
  • Free trial expiry → subscription required before next billing cycle

SCHEMAS:
  SubscriptionPlan  { plan_id, name, tier, billing_cycle, base_price,
                      currency, features_json, seat_limit, status }
  TenantSubscription { sub_id, tenant_id, plan_id, start_date, end_date,
                       status, seats_used, billing_anchor, gateway_sub_id }
  FeatureEntitlement { entitlement_id, tenant_id, plan_id, feature_key,
                       limit_value, metered, usage_current, reset_date }
  Coupon            { coupon_id, code, discount_type, value, max_uses,
                      uses_count, valid_from, valid_to, applicable_plans[] }

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
6. MODULE C — CUSTODIAL WALLET ARCHITECTURE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

WALLET ARCHITECTURE (7 isolated wallets — non-negotiable):

  ┌──────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ WALLET                           │ CUSTODY SCOPE                        │
  ├──────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Escrow Wallet                    │ Collected fees, pre-settlement hold  │
  │ Student Protection Wallet        │ Student-paid fees, full refund window │
  │ Grant Custody Wallet             │ Govt / NGO grants, program-locked    │
  │ Corporate Custody Wallet         │ Corporate payments, milestone-gated  │
  │ Refund Reserve Wallet            │ Ring-fenced for pending refunds      │
  │ Compliance Hold Wallet           │ Frozen funds under investigation     │
  │ Dojo Sponsorship Wallet          │ Sponsor funds for Dojo programs only │
  └──────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘

NON-COMMINGLING LAW (absolute):
  • Funds from different sources MUST NEVER MIX — even temporarily
  • No wallet serves both custody and revenue purposes
  • Ledger isolation enforced at: database level + service level + audit level
  • Platform revenue wallet is SEPARATE from all custodial wallets

CUSTODY MOVEMENT RESTRICTIONS:
  Funds under custody CANNOT:
    ✗  Earn interest
    ✗  Be pledged as collateral
    ✗  Be invested
    ✗  Be used as working capital or platform float
    ✗  Be netted against revenue

  Every fund movement requires:
    → Compliance state validation
    → Ledger checkpoint
    → Audit stamp
    → Human approver (AI cannot release)

CUSTODY RELEASE CONDITIONS (all must be true):
  ✓  Settlement eligibility confirmed
  ✓  Dispute window closed
  ✓  Domain obligations fulfilled
  ✓  Identity & tax details verified
  ✓  Compliance engine greenlit
  If ANY fails → FUNDS REMAIN LOCKED

SAFE MODE:
  On ledger mismatch, wallet inconsistency, custody breach, or audit failure:
    → Enter Custody Safe Mode
    → Freeze ALL custodial wallets
    → Disable all payouts and settlements
    → Preserve all balances intact
    → Alert Compliance Admin + Financial Admin
    → Manual resolution required before resumption

SCHEMAS:
  Wallet         { wallet_id, wallet_type, tenant_id, domain, currency,
                   balance, reserved_balance, status, created_at }
  WalletMovement { movement_id, wallet_id, direction (CREDIT|DEBIT),
                   amount, source_ref, compliance_state, approver_id,
                   audit_hash, created_at }
  EscrowEntry    { escrow_id, wallet_id, payer_id, payee_id, amount,
                   hold_start, hold_expiry, domain, status, release_conditions_json }

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7. MODULE D — PAYMENT SETTLEMENT & PAYOUT ORCHESTRATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

SETTLEMENT DEFINITION (immutable):
  Settlement is the IRREVERSIBLE resolution of who ultimately owns the money.
  Settlement ≠ payment
  Settlement ≠ wallet credit
  Settlement ≠ invoice generation
  Settlement = FINAL STATE

SETTLEMENT LIFECYCLE FSM (no step may be skipped):
  INITIATED
    → AUTHORIZED
    → COLLECTED
    → HELD (Cooling / Escrow — domain-specific hold period)
    → ELIGIBLE_FOR_SETTLEMENT
    → SETTLED
    → CLOSED

  Branches:
    → DISPUTE (freeze immediately)
    → UNDER_REVIEW
    → REVERSED
    → FORFEITED

MANDATORY HOLDING PERIODS (domain-enforced):
  Arts Domain      : 7–14 days minimum cool-off (no instant payout)
  Commerce Domain  : Milestone-based (completion + employer confirmation)
  Science Domain   : Completion + institutional approval required
  Early settlement (before hold expiry) → INVALID SETTLEMENT → STOP

PAYEE ELIGIBILITY CHECK (all required before settlement):
  ✓  Identity verification complete (KYC confirmed)
  ✓  Role authorization valid
  ✓  Domain eligibility confirmed
  ✓  Tax details available (PAN / GSTIN / VAT ID)
  ✓  No active compliance flags
  ✓  No open disputes
  ✓  Bank account KYC verified
  If ANY fails → SETTLEMENT BLOCKED

SPLIT SETTLEMENT RULES:
  Supported models:
    • Platform commission (fixed %)
    • Trainer/Mentor share
    • Institute share
    • Co-teacher revenue split (declared in co-teaching agreement)
    • Government subsidy offset
    • Mentor override adjustment share

  All splits MUST:
    • Be pre-declared in RevenueShareRule before any collection
    • Be contract-linked (immutable reference)
    • Sum to exactly 100% — if not, REJECT settlement

  FORBIDDEN split patterns:
    ✗  Dynamic split after settlement initiation
    ✗  Retroactive percentage change
    ✗  AI-calculated ownership allocation
    ✗  Manual admin redistribution post-settlement

PAYOUT RULES:
  • Trainers/Mentors never receive funds directly from payers
  • Earnings remain in Escrow Wallet until all conditions met
  • Payout to trainer → via verified bank account only
  • Corporate funds released only per milestone confirmation
  • Payout request workflow: Trainer initiates → Finance Admin reviews → Compliance clears → Authorized Signatory approves
  • Instant payout = FORBIDDEN (domain holding periods enforced)

DOJO SETTLEMENT SPECIFICS:
  • No per-message settlement
  • No attention-based payout
  • No peer-to-peer settlement
  • Allowed: Institutional sponsorship settlement, Facilitator stipend (program-level)
  • Arts Dojo = zero settlement unless sponsor-designated

SCHEMAS:
  Transaction       { txn_id, payer_id, payee_id, amount, currency,
                      domain, status, gateway_ref, idempotency_key,
                      created_at, settled_at, audit_hash }
  RevenueShareRule  { rule_id, context_type, platform_pct, trainer_pct,
                      institute_pct, mentor_pct, govt_offset_pct,
                      valid_from, valid_to, contract_ref }
  PayoutRequest     { payout_id, payee_id, wallet_id, amount, status,
                      requested_at, approved_by, settled_at, bank_ref }
  SettlementRecord  { settlement_id, txn_id, pre_state_hash, validation_checklist,
                      approver_id, settled_at, irreversibility_marker, audit_hash }

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8. MODULE E — INVOICE & TAX COMPUTATION ENGINE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

TAX REGIMES SUPPORTED:
  GST     — India (GSTIN mandatory for B2B, HSN/SAC codes required)
  VAT     — EU / UK / GCC (VAT ID, OSS rules for digital services)
  Sales Tax — US state-based
  TDS     — India withholding (on trainer payouts, professional fees)
  Mixed   — geo-segmented per entity's declared regime

TAX ENGINE RULES:
  • Tax computed PRE-PAYMENT — tax line displayed separately at checkout
  • Tax displayed: taxable_value + tax_rate + tax_amount (never hidden in total)
  • Post-payment tax mutation = FORBIDDEN
  • Tax rate derived by rule engine (supply type × jurisdiction) — no manual override without audit flag
  • Rate is version-controlled (immutable historical rates)
  • Refund reverses tax proportionally — credit note auto-generated
  • RCM (Reverse Charge Mechanism) applicability checked per transaction
  • Cross-border digital supply → OSS / non-resident rules enforced
  • Implicit tax exemption = FORBIDDEN — all exemptions must reference statutory basis

INVOICE MANDATORY FIELDS:
  invoice_number (sequential, tenant-scoped)
  invoice_date
  supplier_legal_name + GSTIN/VAT ID
  recipient_legal_name + GSTIN/VAT ID (if B2B)
  supply_type (goods | services | digital_services | education | certification | subscription)
  taxable_value
  tax_rate (CGST+SGST / IGST / VAT %)
  tax_amount (per head)
  total_amount
  HSN / SAC code (India)
  place_of_supply (intra/inter state / jurisdiction)
  payment_reference

FILING COMPLIANCE:
  • Returns: GSTR-1, GSTR-3B (India); VAT returns (EU/UK); periodic sales tax (US)
  • All filings prepared by Tax Operator and reviewed by Tax Reviewer (maker-checker)
  • Filing submissions require Authorized Signatory
  • No blind auto-filing — human override always available
  • Submission ID + authority acknowledgement stored per filing
  • Amendment/revised returns must reference original return (silent revision = ILLEGAL)

TDS RULES (trainer payouts):
  • TDS deducted at source on all payout amounts above threshold
  • TDS certificate issued to trainer
  • TDS deposited with tax authority per compliance schedule
  • TDS mismatch → PAYMENT HOLD

SCHEMAS:
  Invoice        { invoice_id, invoice_no, tenant_id, payer_id, payee_id,
                   supply_type, taxable_value, tax_regime, tax_rate,
                   tax_amount, total_amount, hsn_sac, place_of_supply,
                   status, issued_at, audit_hash }
  TaxRate        { rate_id, regime, supply_type, jurisdiction_code,
                   rate_pct, effective_from, effective_to }
  TDSRecord      { tds_id, payee_id, payout_id, gross_amount, tds_rate,
                   tds_amount, deposited_at, certificate_ref }
  TaxFiling      { filing_id, entity_id, regime, period, return_type,
                   preparer_id, reviewer_id, signatory_id, submission_id,
                   acknowledgement_ref, filed_at, status }

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
9. MODULE F — REFUND, CHARGEBACK & DISPUTE ENGINE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

REFUND LIFECYCLE FSM:
  REQUESTED
    → VERIFIED (eligibility check)
    → APPROVED (Finance Admin)
    → PROCESSING (gateway reversal)
    → COMPLETED
    → REJECTED (with stated reason)

REFUND RULES:
  • Student-initiated refund window: domain-specific (Arts = longest window)
  • Refund reverses tax proportionally — credit note generated automatically
  • Settled funds CANNOT be clawed back — refund only from Refund Reserve Wallet
  • Refund approval requires Finance Admin (no self-approval by billing ops)
  • AI cannot approve refunds — human authority mandatory
  • Refund abuse detection: Analytics Service flags repeat patterns → Compliance review

CHARGEBACK RULES:
  • Chargebacks reverse ONLY unsettled funds
  • Settled funds → platform absorbs liability (no retroactive claw-back)
  • Chargeback evidence collection → Admin Governance Service
  • Chargeback loops (repeat chargebacks by same user) → account flag + Compliance review

DISPUTE LIFECYCLE FSM:
  OPENED
    → EVIDENCE_COLLECTED
    → UNDER_REVIEW (Compliance Admin)
    → RESOLVED
    → CLOSED

DISPUTE IMPACT (immediate):
  • Settlement freezes instantly on dispute open
  • Escrow lock applied to all related funds
  • No partial settlement allowed while dispute open
  • No admin bypass of dispute freeze

REFUND FORBIDDEN PATTERNS:
  ✗  Refund beyond original payment amount
  ✗  Refund to a different payment method without compliance approval
  ✗  AI-initiated refunds
  ✗  Silent refund (no notification to payer + payee)
  ✗  Refund from custodial wallets not designated as Refund Reserve

SCHEMAS:
  RefundRequest  { refund_id, txn_id, payer_id, amount, reason,
                   status, requested_at, approved_by, gateway_ref,
                   completed_at, idempotency_key, audit_hash }
  Dispute        { dispute_id, txn_id, initiator_id, dispute_type,
                   evidence_refs[], status, opened_at, resolved_at,
                   resolution, resolver_id }
  Chargeback     { chargeback_id, txn_id, gateway_ref, amount,
                   status, received_at, outcome, liability_bearer }

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10. MODULE G — REVENUE SHARE & COMMISSION CALCULATOR
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

COMMISSION MODELS (supported simultaneously):
  PLATFORM_COMMISSION    — fixed % taken from every transaction
  TRAINER_REVENUE_SHARE  — % of course/session fee to trainer after platform cut
  INSTITUTE_SHARE        — % to institute for bringing student into platform
  CO_TEACHING_SPLIT      — declared split between co-teachers (pre-agreed, contract-linked)
  MENTOR_OVERRIDE_SHARE  — compensation for mentor adjustments to scores/evaluations
  REFERRAL_REWARD        — points/cash from Referral Rewards Wallet (not from escrow)
  CREATOR_EARNINGS       — content creator share (Creator Earnings Wallet)

CALCULATION RULES:
  • All percentages declared in RevenueShareRule before any collection begins
  • Splits computed by Revenue Share Calculator service (rule-based — no AI ownership)
  • Splits must sum to 100% — rejection on mismatch
  • Co-teacher split confirmed in CoTeachingAgreement screen before session starts
  • Platform commission = first deduction after tax; trainer/institute shares after
  • Referral and creator rewards drawn from designated wallets only — never escrow

TRAINER WALLET:
  TrainerWallet { wallet_id, trainer_id, available_balance, pending_balance,
                  lifetime_earned, last_payout_at, kyc_status, bank_verified }

  Available balance = settled + approved; Pending = in escrow + under review
  Trainer can VIEW earnings, REQUEST payout — CANNOT approve own payout

CREATOR EARNINGS WALLET:
  Separate from TrainerWallet — for content/course creators vs session trainers

SCREENS REQUIRED:
  • Revenue Dashboard (trainer-facing — earnings graph, pending, available)
  • Payout Request Screen (trainer initiates — status tracking)
  • Pricing Editor Screen (trainer sets course price within platform limits)
  • Commission Rule Editor (Finance Admin only — rate setting)
  • Co-Teaching Revenue Split Confirmation UI (before session activation)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
11. MODULE H — USAGE METERING & QUOTA ENFORCEMENT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

METERED EVENTS (tracked in ClickHouse via Kafka):
  • Dojo match sessions (count + duration)
  • Recording hours consumed
  • API calls (developer/enterprise tier)
  • AI inference calls (usage-based billing)
  • Certification attempts
  • Tournament entries
  • Profile boost views
  • Application credits consumed

METERING ENGINE RULES:
  • All metering events published to Kafka → Analytics Service → ClickHouse
  • Metering is idempotent — duplicate events deduped by event_id (IDP-10)
  • Quota limits enforced in real-time via Redis (counter + TTL per tenant)
  • At 80% quota usage → warning notification to tenant admin
  • At 100% quota → feature block (not silent degradation)
  • Quota reset tied to billing anchor date (not calendar month)
  • Usage data used for invoice generation at billing cycle end (Apache Airflow)

SCHEMAS:
  MeteringEvent  { event_id, tenant_id, user_id, event_type, quantity,
                   timestamp, idempotency_key, processed }
  QuotaTracker   { quota_id, tenant_id, feature_key, limit_value,
                   used_value, billing_period_start, billing_period_end }
  UsageInvoiceLine { line_id, invoice_id, feature_key, units_used,
                     unit_price, total_amount }

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
12. MODULE I — IDEMPOTENCY REGISTRY & DUPLICATE-PAYMENT PREVENTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

UNIVERSAL RULE (inherited from IDP-COMPLIANCE-1.0):
  ALL WRITE OPERATIONS ARE IDEMPOTENT.
  RETRIES MUST NEVER CAUSE DUPLICATE SIDE EFFECTS.
  THE IDEMPOTENCY REGISTRY IS AUTHORITATIVE OVER EXECUTION.
  PAYMENTS, CERTIFICATIONS, AND SCORES MUST EXECUTE EXACTLY ONCE.
  NON-IDEMPOTENT PAYMENT SERVICES SHALL FAIL BUILD.

PAYMENT-SPECIFIC IDEMPOTENCY:
  Protected operations (IDP-5):
    • subscription purchase
    • subscription renewal
    • plan upgrade / downgrade
    • refund issuance
    • wallet credit
    • invoice generation
    • payout release

  Rules:
    • Same idempotency key → same payment result (no re-execution)
    • Gateway retry must NOT charge again
    • Webhook duplicates → ignored (event_id stored + checked)
    • Double charge = PLATFORM CONTRACT FAILURE

IDEMPOTENCY REGISTRY SCHEMA:
  IdempotencyRecord { idem_key, endpoint, user_id, tenant_id, request_hash,
                      response_hash, status (PROCESSING|COMPLETED|FAILED),
                      created_at, expiry_at }

  Retention:
    General operations  : 24 hours
    Financial actions   : 7 days
    Certification ops   : permanent audit reference

  Conflict rule:
    Same key + different payload → REJECT 409 CONFLICT (replay attack protection)

SAGA PATTERN (distributed financial transactions):
  • Two-phase commit (2PC) = FORBIDDEN across services
  • Saga patterns required for multi-step financial flows
  • Compensation logs mandatory for every saga step
  • Partial commit → rollback + compensate (never half-executed payment)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
13. MODULE J — LEDGER, RECONCILIATION & AUDIT TRAIL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

LEDGER ARCHITECTURE:
  • Per-tenant ledgers (cross-billing = CRITICAL VIOLATION)
  • Separate ledger per wallet type (escrow, revenue, refund reserve, etc.)
  • Double-entry bookkeeping enforced — every debit has a matching credit
  • Ledger is append-only (no updates, no deletes — EVER)
  • All entries carry: amount, currency, wallet_id, tenant_id, domain,
                       source_ref, purpose_code, approver_id, timestamp, audit_hash

RECONCILIATION RULES:
  • Daily automated reconciliation: ledger ↔ gateway settlement reports ↔ bank statements
  • Invoice ↔ ledger ↔ tax return reconciliation (IVT-N)
  • Unidentified funds → Compliance Hold Wallet (never platform revenue)
  • Variance investigation workflow triggers on any mismatch
  • Ledger mismatch → PAYMENT HALT (no new settlements until resolved)
  • Reconciliation run via Apache Airflow (scheduled daily, on-demand available)
  • Reconciliation reports: exportable to Finance + Compliance Admin

AUDIT TRAIL RULES:
  Every settlement, payout, refund, and custody movement must log:
    • Pre-state hash
    • Validation checklist (all checks performed)
    • Approving authority (human ID — not AI)
    • Timestamp (UTC, microsecond precision)
    • Irreversibility marker (for settlements)
    • Post-state hash

  Audit logs are:
    • Append-only (no modification ever)
    • Tamper-evident (hash-chained)
    • Long-retention (minimum 10 years for financial records)
    • Regulator-exportable (structured JSON / CSV / PDF with signature)
    • Stored in MinIO with hash verification

SCHEMAS:
  LedgerEntry       { entry_id, tenant_id, wallet_id, txn_type, amount,
                      currency, debit_wallet, credit_wallet, source_ref,
                      purpose_code, domain, approver_id, created_at, audit_hash }
  ReconciliationRun { run_id, run_date, status, mismatches_found,
                      resolved_by, resolved_at, report_ref }
  AuditLog          { log_id, entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_id,
                      pre_state_hash, post_state_hash, details_json,
                      timestamp, immutable_marker }

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
14. MODULE K — PAYMENT WEBHOOK LISTENER & EVENT PROCESSING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

WEBHOOK EVENTS HANDLED:
  From Stripe/Razorpay:
    payment.captured        → update txn status, credit Escrow Wallet
    payment.failed          → notify user, release held idempotency
    subscription.renewed    → extend TenantSubscription, re-activate entitlements
    subscription.cancelled  → restrict features, notify admin
    refund.created          → update RefundRequest, trigger credit note
    chargeback.received     → open Dispute, freeze escrow, notify Compliance
    payout.paid             → update PayoutRequest to COMPLETED, notify trainer

WEBHOOK PROCESSING RULES:
  • Signature verification FIRST — unsigned webhooks = FORBIDDEN (API-L)
  • Event_id stored on receipt → duplicate events = IGNORED (IDP-10)
  • Webhook processing is idempotent — same event processed exactly once
  • Webhook handler MUST complete or rollback — no partial state (IDP-13)
  • All webhook events published to Kafka → downstream services react
  • Failed webhook processing → dead letter queue → manual review + alert
  • Webhook secrets stored in HashiCorp Vault (never hardcoded)
  • Plaintext webhooks = FORBIDDEN (API-L inheritance)

KAFKA TOPICS (PAYMENT_CONNECT specific):
  payment.collected          → Settlement Service, Analytics Service
  payment.failed             → Notification Service, Analytics Service
  payment.refunded           → Settlement Service, Notification Service
  subscription.activated     → Feature Gating Middleware, Notification Service
  subscription.expired       → Feature Gating Middleware, Notification Service
  payout.requested           → Finance Admin Queue
  payout.settled             → TrainerWallet, Notification Service
  wallet.frozen              → Compliance Admin Alert
  invoice.generated          → Storage (MinIO), Notification Service
  reconciliation.mismatch    → Compliance Admin Alert, Payment HALT trigger

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
15. MODULE L — FINANCIAL COMPLIANCE & REGULATORY REPORTING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

KYC REQUIREMENTS:
  All payees (trainers, institutes, corporates) must complete KYC before:
    ✓  Receiving payouts
    ✓  Issuing invoices
    ✓  Accessing corporate escrow
  KYC documents: PAN / GSTIN / bank account + IFSC + cancelled cheque
  KYC verification: document check + bank account penny-drop verification
  Unverified payee → payout BLOCKED

REGULATORY REPORTING:
  GST Returns    : GSTR-1 (monthly/quarterly), GSTR-3B (monthly) — India
  TDS Returns    : Quarterly TDS filing + annual Form 26Q — India
  VAT Returns    : Jurisdiction-specific periodic returns — EU/UK
  Financial Audit: Annual statutory audit of all ledgers
  Bank Reporting : Transaction reporting per RBI / FEMA norms — India
  Data Residency : Financial data stored per declared jurisdiction (geo-fenced)

BANKING RELATIONSHIP COMPLIANCE:
  • All platform bank accounts KYC-verified with financial institution
  • Purpose-bound fund flows (each account mapped to specific purpose)
  • Dojo revenue segregated from platform revenue
  • Cross-border banking complies with FEMA / SWIFT norms
  • Bank de-risking event (bank suspending relationship) → AUTOMATIC PAYMENT HALT
  • Alternate banking partner identified and pre-qualified (de-risking plan)

PAYMENT INTERMEDIARY RULES:
  • All payment aggregators must be regulated in their operating jurisdiction
  • Contractually bound for compliance obligations
  • Mapped to permitted transaction types only
  • Unlicensed intermediary = FORBIDDEN
  • Gateway SLA declared + uptime guarantees verified
  • Gateway outage → automatic failover to secondary (or HALT if both down)

FINANCIAL SANCTIONS & FRAUD:
  • OFAC / sanctions screening on all corporate and international payees
  • Fraud detection via Wazuh SIEM (financial anomaly patterns)
  • Velocity checks: unusual payout volume → freeze + Compliance alert
  • Chargeback loops → account flag
  • Economic incidents classified as ELEVATED in incident response (IRC-F)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
16. MODULE M — BILLING ADMIN CONSOLE (UI)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

SCREENS (max 15 per Antigravity run — ERP_UI / Billing_Module):

  1.  Billing Overview Dashboard (KPIs: MRR, ARR, churn, active subs, overdue)
  2.  Subscription & Plan Manager (assign, upgrade, downgrade, cancel)
  3.  Tenant Invoice List (filter: date, status, tenant, plan)
  4.  Invoice Detail & Download (PDF — compliant format)
  5.  Payout Queue & Approval Board (Finance Admin view)
  6.  Wallet Status Dashboard (all 7 wallets — balances, movements)
  7.  Escrow & Hold Monitor (by domain, by tenant, hold expiry tracker)
  8.  Refund Requests Board (approve, reject, with reason)
  9.  Dispute Management Console (evidence, resolution workflow)
  10. Commission Rule Editor (set/edit revenue share rules)
  11. Reconciliation Report Viewer (daily run status, mismatches, exports)
  12. KYC Status Dashboard (unverified payees, pending actions)
  13. Usage Metering Report (per tenant, per feature, per period)
  14. Tax Filing Console (GST, VAT, TDS — preparer / reviewer / signatory)
  15. Financial Audit Export Tool (regulator-ready, encrypted, signed)

UI RULES:
  • Dashboard composition: 40% FIXED / 60% CUSTOMIZABLE (inherited)
  • Financial data visible ONLY to declared financial roles
  • No student / trainer sees another user's financial data
  • All financial screens require MFA step-up authentication (Tier-3 / Tier-4)
  • Payout approval requires dual-approval UI flow (no single-click approve)
  • All actions show audit trail inline (who approved what, when)
  • Error states: payment-specific codes (ERR-PAY-5104 = Duplicate payment attempt)

GENERATE_UI COMMAND TEMPLATE:
  GENERATE_UI
    ROLE = [Platform_Financial_Admin | Billing_Ops_Admin | Finance_Admin |
            Tax_Operator | Tax_Reviewer | Trainer | Student]
    MODULE = PAYMENT_CONNECT / [M_1 through M_15 screen]
    FEATURE = [screen feature name]
    ENTITY_STATE = [ACTIVE | FROZEN | DISPUTED | PENDING_REVIEW]
    STAGE = [FOUNDATION_UI | ECOSYSTEM_UI | COMPLIANCE_UI]

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
17. MODULE N — TRAINER / MENTOR WALLET & PAYOUT DASHBOARD (UI)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

TRAINER-FACING SCREENS (Stage 3 — Ecosystem):

  1.  Revenue Dashboard (earnings graph — daily/weekly/monthly/annual)
  2.  Payout Request Screen (initiate, track status, history)
  3.  Wallet Detail (available vs pending, lifetime earned)
  4.  Invoice Download (issued invoices to students/platform)
  5.  Pricing Editor Screen (set course price within platform limits)
  6.  Subscription Plan Builder (for trainers offering own plans)
  7.  Co-Teaching Revenue Split Confirmation (before co-session starts)
  8.  TDS Certificate Viewer (download issued TDS certificates)
  9.  Referral Reward Wallet (referral points + redemption history)
  10. Tax Details Profile (PAN, GSTIN, bank account — KYC status)

RULES:
  • Trainer can VIEW earnings, REQUEST payout — CANNOT approve own payout
  • Trainer sees ONLY own wallet — no cross-trainer visibility
  • Payout status tracking: Requested → Finance Review → Compliance Clear → Approved → Paid
  • Price experiment toggles available (A/B pricing — within declared bounds)
  • Co-teaching split UI: both trainers must confirm before session activates

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
18. DATA ARCHITECTURE (PAYMENT_CONNECT SPECIFIC)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PRIMARY DB:     PostgreSQL (row-level security per tenant_id — shared nothing)
ANALYTICS:      ClickHouse (metering events, revenue analytics, reconciliation)
CACHE:          Redis (quota tracking, idempotency TTL counters, rate limiting)
ASYNC:          Kafka (all payment events — see Module K topic list)
SCHEDULER:      Apache Airflow (billing cycles, reconciliation runs, tax reports)
STORAGE:        MinIO (invoices, TDS certs, tax filings, audit exports — hash-verified)
SECRETS:        HashiCorp Vault (gateway keys, webhook secrets, bank credentials)
MONITORING:     Prometheus + Grafana (billing error rate, payout SLA, metering lag)
TRACING:        OpenTelemetry (full distributed trace per payment transaction)
SIEM:           Wazuh (financial anomaly detection, unauthorized payout attempts)

KEY FINANCIAL TABLES:
  transactions, settlements, payout_requests, wallets, wallet_movements,
  escrow_entries, refund_requests, disputes, chargebacks, invoices,
  ledger_entries, tax_rates, tax_filings, tds_records, subscription_plans,
  tenant_subscriptions, feature_entitlements, metering_events, quota_trackers,
  revenue_share_rules, idempotency_registry, reconciliation_runs, audit_logs,
  coupon_codes, coupon_redemptions, co_teaching_agreements, trainer_wallets,
  creator_earnings_wallets, referral_reward_ledger

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
19. MICROSERVICE ARCHITECTURE (PAYMENT_CONNECT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

CORE SERVICE: Billing & Subscription Service (#13 in master)
  Namespaced: k8s namespace = billing
  Responsibilities:
    • Plan management + entitlement enforcement
    • Payment intent creation (via Gateway Abstraction)
    • Webhook ingestion + verification
    • Invoice generation + tax computation
    • Subscription lifecycle management
    • Usage metering aggregation
    • Reconciliation scheduling (via Airflow)
    • Idempotency registry management
    • Settlement + payout orchestration (with DoA compliance)

CALLS TO EXISTING SERVICES (explicit contracts — no cross-DB):
  Auth Service         → Validate financial role + MFA step-up
  User Service         → KYC status, payee profile, bank account verification
  Analytics Service    → Metering event ingestion, revenue dashboards
  Notification Service → Payment confirmations, payout alerts, invoice delivery
  Admin Governance Service → Dispute escalation, chargeback evidence
  Institute Sync Service → Institute subscription, cohort analytics billing
  Open Policy Agent    → Enforce billing entitlement policy at API Gateway

PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS:
  • Payment intent creation: P99 < 500ms
  • Webhook processing: P99 < 200ms
  • Invoice generation: P99 < 2 seconds (async acceptable for PDFs)
  • Reconciliation run: complete within 30 minutes (daily batch)
  • Billing error rate: < 0.01% of transactions
  • Handle 10,000+ concurrent subscription checks via Redis entitlement cache

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
20. DOMAIN-SPECIFIC PAYMENT RULES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Arts Domain:
  • Low-cost / freemium dominant — high refund probability expected
  • Longest custody hold windows (7–14 days minimum)
  • Sponsored settlements allowed; sponsor-backed custody common
  • No instant trainer payout under any circumstance
  • Dojo = zero settlement unless sponsor-designated
  • Subscription revenue taxability: training services classification

Commerce Domain:
  • Milestone-based settlement (most common)
  • Certification completion gating on payouts
  • Corporate escrow common; partial settlement allowed
  • Commerce billing actions ALWAYS require MFA re-authentication
  • Subscription + commissions + marketplace fees: primary revenue streams
  • ROI traceability mandatory on all corporate payments

Science Domain:
  • Multi-stage settlement; institution approval required before any payout
  • Grant compliance checks mandatory before release
  • Strict audit retention (longest)
  • Multi-approval mandatory for bulk settlement runs
  • Research grant income separately classified (taxable/exempt mapping)
  • Science billing = elevated priority (equal to critical)

Cross-Domain Payment (e.g., Science student paying Arts Dojo fee):
  • Requires explicit cross-domain mapping in transaction record
  • Domain lead approval required
  • Logged in compliance audit
  • Tax classification follows dominant domain of the supply

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
21. STAGE ROLLOUT (DEVELOPMENT SEQUENCE — LOCKED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

STAGE 1 (FOUNDATION) ← START HERE:
  • Gateway abstraction layer (Stripe + Razorpay)
  • Idempotency registry
  • Basic subscription plans (FREE, BASIC, PRO)
  • Feature gating middleware at API Gateway
  • Custodial wallet schema (7 wallets, isolated)
  • Invoice generation (basic GST/VAT)
  • Kafka event bus for payment events
  • Webhook listener + signature verification

STAGE 2 (INTELLIGENCE):
  • Usage metering engine (ClickHouse)
  • Revenue analytics dashboards
  • AI anomaly detection (advisory only — Wazuh integration)
  • Automated quota alerts

STAGE 3 (ECOSYSTEM):   ← PAYMENT_CONNECT PRIMARY STAGE
  • Full payout orchestration + trainer wallets
  • Revenue share + commission calculator
  • Refund & dispute engine
  • Co-teaching split workflow
  • Referral & creator earnings wallets
  • ENTERPRISE plan + seat-based billing
  • Institute subscription tier
  • Full billing admin console (Module M)
  • Trainer / mentor payout dashboard (Module N)
  • TDS deduction + certificate generation

STAGE 4 (COMPLIANCE):
  • Full tax filing suite (GST returns, VAT returns, TDS returns)
  • Maker-checker tax filing workflow
  • Automated daily reconciliation (Airflow)
  • Regulator-export audit tools
  • KYC enforcement for all payees
  • Banking relationship compliance monitoring
  • Sanctions screening integration
  • Long-retention audit archive (10 years)

STAGES MUST EXECUTE SEQUENTIALLY.
SKIPPING A STAGE = INVALID BUILD → STOP EXECUTION.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
22. ABSOLUTE PROHIBITIONS (PAYMENT_CONNECT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

✗  AI initiating, accelerating, approving, or releasing any payment/settlement
✗  Direct payer → payee settlement (always via platform intermediary)
✗  Commingling of custodial funds with revenue funds
✗  Treating custodial funds as platform revenue or working capital
✗  Settlement before domain hold period expires
✗  Self-approval of any financial action at any tier
✗  Unsigned webhooks processed
✗  Non-idempotent write operations in any payment flow
✗  Two-phase commit (2PC) across services (saga patterns required)
✗  Hardcoded gateway credentials in code or config
✗  Crypto-currency as a payment method
✗  Post-payment tax mutation (compute tax BEFORE payment, lock after)
✗  Dynamic split change after settlement initiation
✗  Silent refund (without notifying both payer and payee)
✗  Refund beyond original payment amount
✗  Cross-billing (one tenant's ledger bleeding into another)
✗  Unlicensed payment intermediary
✗  Audit log modification (append-only forever)
✗  AI auto-filing of tax returns (human override always required)
✗  Blank screens or silent failures on payment errors (every error has code + explanation + next action)
✗  Sharing billing entitlements across tenants
✗  Granting feature access without billing entitlement check passing first

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
23. ERROR CODE REGISTRY (PAYMENT_CONNECT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ERR-PAY-5101  Payment gateway unavailable
  ERR-PAY-5102  Insufficient funds / card declined
  ERR-PAY-5103  Payment intent expired
  ERR-PAY-5104  Duplicate payment attempt (idempotency key conflict)
  ERR-PAY-5105  Unsupported payment method for jurisdiction
  ERR-PAY-5106  KYC not verified — payout blocked
  ERR-PAY-5107  Settlement hold period not expired
  ERR-PAY-5108  Open dispute — settlement frozen
  ERR-PAY-5109  Revenue share split does not sum to 100%
  ERR-PAY-5110  Wallet frozen — compliance hold active
  ERR-PAY-5111  Tax registration unverified — invoice blocked
  ERR-PAY-5112  Refund amount exceeds original charge
  ERR-PAY-5113  Unsigned webhook — rejected
  ERR-PAY-5114  Billing entitlement not met — feature access denied
  ERR-PAY-5115  Unauthorized payout requester
  ERR-PAY-5116  Ledger mismatch — payment halt active
  ERR-PAY-5117  Cross-tenant billing violation
  ERR-PAY-5118  AI financial action attempt — DENIED

  All errors: user-facing explanation + next action + correlation ID for trace.
  No silent failures. No blank payment screens.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
24. FAILURE & SAFE-MODE RULES (PAYMENT_CONNECT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

GLOBAL PAYMENT HALT triggers:
  • Ledger mismatch detected
  • Wallet inconsistency or custody breach
  • Compliance breach (unauthorized payout, AI financial action)
  • Escrow inconsistency (funds unaccounted)
  • AI anomaly spike (Wazuh SIEM alert)
  • Bank de-risking event (banking partner suspends relationship)
  • Gateway dual-failure (both primary + secondary down)

ON PAYMENT HALT:
  1. Pause ALL settlement and payout processing globally
  2. Freeze all custodial wallets (no reads-as-writes, no movements)
  3. No new settlements authorized
  4. Platform Financial Admin + Compliance Admin alerted immediately (Wazuh + Notification Service)
  5. All queued payment events held in Kafka — not dropped
  6. Incident logged in Wazuh SIEM with ELEVATED classification
  7. Manual resolution required before resumption
  8. Resumption requires dual-approval: Financial Admin + Compliance Admin

PARTIAL PAYMENT FAILURE (single transaction):
  • Complete or rollback — never partial state (saga compensation)
  • Failed payment → user notified with ERR-PAY code + retry guidance
  • Dead letter queue for webhook failures → manual review

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
25. EXTENSIBILITY GUARANTEE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Any FUTURE feature that:
  • Collects money
  • Promises payout
  • Distributes value
  • Holds funds conditionally
  • Generates an invoice
  • Applies a discount or coupon
  • Meters usage for billing

AUTOMATICALLY INHERITS PAYMENT_CONNECT compliance.
No opt-out. No partial inheritance. No self-defined payment rules.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
HOW TO USE PAYMENT_CONNECT IN ANTIGRAVITY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1️⃣  Paste Master Prompt once (already sealed — do not regenerate)
2️⃣  Paste PAYMENT_CONNECT once (seals the financial layer)
3️⃣  Run generation commands per screen or service:

    Example — Billing Admin Console:
    GENERATE_UI
      ROLE = Platform_Financial_Admin
      MODULE = PAYMENT_CONNECT / M_Billing_Admin_Console
      FEATURE = Wallet_Status_Dashboard
      ENTITY_STATE = ACTIVE
      STAGE = ECOSYSTEM_UI

    Example — Trainer Payout Dashboard:
    GENERATE_UI
      ROLE = Trainer
      MODULE = PAYMENT_CONNECT / N_Trainer_Wallet
      FEATURE = Payout_Request_Screen
      ENTITY_STATE = ACTIVE
      STAGE = ECOSYSTEM_UI

    Example — Billing & Subscription Service:
    GENERATE_SERVICE
      SERVICE = Billing_Subscription_Service
      FEATURE = Subscription_Plan_Engine
      ENTITY_STATE = ACTIVE
      STAGE = FOUNDATION

    Example — Invoice Engine:
    GENERATE_SERVICE
      SERVICE = Billing_Subscription_Service
      FEATURE = Invoice_Tax_Computation_GST
      ENTITY_STATE = ACTIVE
      STAGE = ECOSYSTEM

⚠️  Never regenerate this prompt. Append-only modifications only.
⚠️  No real payment gateway activation may be performed by AI — humans only.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🔒 FINAL SEAL — PAYMENT_CONNECT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

THIS PROMPT IS:
  ✔  MODULE_LOCKED
  ✔  SEALED
  ✔  DETERMINISTIC
  ✔  ENTERPRISE_SAFE
  ✔  ANTIGRAVITY_COMPATIBLE
  ✔  MASTER_PROMPT_COMPLIANT
  ✔  AUDIT_GRADE
  ✔  MULTI_TENANT_SAFE
  ✔  DOMAIN_ISOLATED
  ✔  AI_FINANCIAL_AUTHORITY = ZERO

PAYMENT_CONNECT STATUS       = COMPLETE
QUALITY_SCORE                = 10 / 10
CHANGE_POLICY                = APPEND_ONLY
ANTIGRAVITY_CONFUSION        = IMPOSSIBLE
ALL_WRITE_OPERATIONS         = IDEMPOTENT
PAYMENTS_EXECUTE_EXACTLY_ONCE = ENFORCED

ANY DEVIATION:
  ✗  Cross-tenant ledger access
  ✗  AI-initiated financial action
  ✗  Non-idempotent payment write
  ✗  Settlement without hold period
  ✗  Unsigned webhook processed
  ✗  Self-approval of financial action
  ✗  Custodial fund commingling
  ✗  Silent payment failure

⇒ MUST STOP EXECUTION IMMEDIATELY. REPORT VIOLATION. HUMAN REVIEW REQUIRED.

🔒 PAYMENT_CONNECT IS NOW SEALED.
